segment-text: Story by
and Lena Waithe
INT. DINER - NIGHT 1 (SOMEWHERE IN OHIO)

We're in the kind of place that feels like everyone should know your name. But they don't. Families frequent this spot when they've fallen on hard times. It's practically empty on this boring Thursday. SERVERS drop off hot plates while ELDERLY PEOPLE sip cheap coffee at the counter. Our focus is directed toward a BLACK MAN and a BLACK WOMAN sitting in a small booth in the back. The MAN: has a slender frame and a laid back demeanor. He's not a fan of rocking the boat or ruffling feathers, but he ain't no punk either. For the purpose of this story we'll call him SLIM. The WOMAN: She's regal as fuck. She's not an easy laugh and she's always waiting for the other shoe to drop. For the purposes of this story, we'll call her QUEEN. Not unlike most first dates there are long silences and a lot of forced small talk. The WAITRESS quickly drops off their food and walks away without uttering a word. Queen examines the spread. Slim prays over his food. This is how he was raised. Queen sits awkwardly waiting for him to look up. As soon as he does -QUEEN
SLIM She clocks the two sunny side up eggs on his plate.
It's all good.
No, it's not.
It's just eggs.
I have a thing about that. Eggs?
SLIM
No, about people not doing their job well.
She's got four kids and an
alcoholic husband at home. She's doing the best she can.
How do you know that?
She comes through my line every Sunday, yelling at her kids and giving me a stack of coupons. She can never find her Costco card so I always let her use mine so she can get the discount.
That's nice of you.
Can I ask you something?
I don't know, can you?
Easy, I know good grammar. Do you?
We not in a classroom.
They have a quick standoff.
You gonna ask me your question or not?
What took you so long to respond to me?
I didn't realize that much time had
I sent you a very well crafted message three weeks ago. I spell checked it and everything and got crickets. Then today, out of the blue, you hit me up asking if I wanna grab dinner. What changed?
I had a shitty day.
What was so shitty about it?
The state decided to execute my client.
Queen says that as if she were giving him the logline to her
In an instant Slim loses his appetite.
I didn't know that was still legal here.
Yup. It's legal in 31 states. And Ohio happens to be one of 'em.
Was he innocent?
Even if he's not - the state shouldn't decide whether he lives or dies. I'm sorry.
SLIM
Me too. (then) Normally, I'd just go home and have a glass of wine by myself. But I didn't feel like being alone. Not tonight.
Slim can't tell if this is a cry for help or a proposition.
You didn't have any friends or family you could call? No.
So you turned to Tinder? Yeah.
Slim sips his soda.
Why'd you pick me?
I liked your picture. SLIM (flattered) Word?
You had this sad look on your face. I felt sorry for you. Damn.
SLIM
I didn't mean for that to sound shady.
Well it did.
My dad took that picture of me.
Why aren't you smiling? Slim shrugs. Not ready to divulge the reason just yet.
They're still getting to know each other.
To be honest, I don't have that many photos of myself. Why not?
I know what I look like.
Pictures aren't just about vanity. They're proof of your existence.
My family knows I exist. That's enough. The idea of not having to prove oneself to the world is foreign to Queen.
Slim stares at Queen's plate. Most of her food is untouched. Particularly her Caesar salad.
You gon eat that?
You can have some.
He reaches over and stabs a few big pieces of lettuce with his fork.
They always use just the right amount of dressing.
Do you really like this place or is it the only spot you could afford?
It's black owned. She wasn't expecting that answer. Queen can appreciate this small token of righteousness. Touche.
INT. CAR - AN HOUR LATER - NIGHT 1

Slim drives a WHITE HONDA ACCORD. Yeah, he don't give a fuck about cars. He tries to create a vibe by playing some neosoul music. Which is regular soul music with a hint of patchouli. He keeps looking down at his phone. Checking the directions.
You shouldn't text and drive.
Ain't nobody texting. I'm making sure I don't get lost.
She grabs Slim's phone from his lap.
I can tell you how to get to my hotel.
Give me my phone!
Relax. I'm gonna give it back.
You gon tell me where to turn? Yeah.
Queen points left.
Oh so now you gon point the directions?
I'm telling you where to go.
Slim is visibly annoyed. Queen starts sifting through his phone.
You made a playlist? That's cute.
Slim tries to grab his phone back, unsuccessfully.
Don't go through my phone!
She smiles.
Did you like "In a Sentimental Mood" before or after "Love Jones"?
I knew about that shit way before
Don't lie.
Okay - I fucked with it more after I saw the movie.
I appreciate your honesty. Then -SLIM
So what happens tonight?
You're gonna drop me off and you'll go home, I guess. Oh.
SLIM
Did you think we were gonna have sex?
Slim is embarrassed.
No. I thought we were gonna hang
out. Maybe get to know each other.
Nah, I'm good.

Slim takes his phone back from Queen causing him to SWERVE a bit. Then he SWITCHES LANES without signaling. Just as he starts to pick up speed. He hears a horrifying sound. WOOP WOOP. Every black person's worst nightmare. We hear a robotic voice muffled through a loud speaker: "Pull over. That corner right there." Slim's heart starts to race. Queen's heart beats at its usual speed.
Do you have any warrants? Really? Do you?
SLIM
Nah, I ain't got no warrants.
Do you have any weapons in the car? Any narcotics?
I got a pocket knife in the glove compartment.
That's fine.
I know it is.
EXT. STREET - NIGHT 1
The POLICE OFFICER steps out of his car and makes his way to Slim's door. He motions for Slim to roll down his window. He does.
Can you turn the radio down?

Slim pauses for a beat. Then he does as he's told. He's always been good at being obedient. The Police Officer aims the light directly in Slim's eyes. Slim quickly shields his face so he can see what's in front of him.
Put your hand down.
He does as he's told.
He only had it up because you were
shining a light in his eyes.
I'm trying to see inside the vehicle.
It's just us.
License and registration.
Slim reaches for his wallet.
I'm grabbing my wallet.
I can see that.
(under her breath) Well, y'all like to shoot first and ask questions later.
Do you have something you wanna say to me?
No, I don't.

Slim hands over his license. Slowly. As he reaches over to rummage through his glove compartment his hand accidentally grazes Queen's thigh. He's not being fresh. Just nervous. He finally finds his crumpled up registration and hands it over.
Do you know why I pulled you over? No, sir.
SLIM
You forgot to use your signal back there. And you swerved a bit.
Queen shakes her head.
Oh, my bad.
Yeah, it is.
The Police Officer takes Slim's license and registration and slowly walks back to his squad car. Queen sits there. Seething. Slim is calm as a cucumber.
I can't believe this shit.
Just chill.
She doesn't like being told what to do.
I told you to chill out.
I have a right to be angry.
I ain't tryna die tonight.
The weight of that statement leaps out of Slim's soul and lands in Queen's lap.
INT. SQUAD CAR - CONTINUOUS - NIGHT 1

The Police Officer talks to his DISPATCHER. On his dashboard we see a PHOTO. It's a picture of his WIFE and KIDS. The Kids are TWIN BOYS. They both have blonde hair and green eyes. They're completely unaware of how privileged their lives will be simply because they were born white and male. The Wife is a bored brunette whose dreams are no bigger than her backyard.
Whattya got? POLICE OFFICER Black male. Black female. Both late twenties. They're in a white Honda. (MORE)
Driver changed lanes without signaling. He was driving erratically.
Is he under the influence?
I don't know yet. I'll make him do a breathalyzer.
Let me know if you need anything. Will do.
INT. SLIM'S CAR - MOMENTS LATER - NIGHT 1
The Officer is back at Slim's window - leaning in a little too close.
Will you step out of the vehicle,
(under her breath) Shit.
Can I ask why, Officer?
No, you can't. Step out of the car.

Before he gets out of the car Queen and Slim lock eyes. He doesn't know Queen very well, but he can tell she has the spirit of Nat Turner wrestling inside of her. Slim steps out and puts his hands on the roof of the car. The Police Officer proceeds to search him, aggressively.
You got any sharp objects in your pockets I should know about? No, sir.
SLIM
Any illegal substances? No, sir.
SLIM
What about in the vehicle? No, sir.
SLIM
INT. SLIM'S CAR - PASSENGER SEAT - SAME TIME - NIGHT 1
Queen's left knee bounces uncontrollably. Her chest goes up and down. Queen looks in the rearview mirror and sees the Police Officer interrogating Slim on the side of the road.
EXT. STREET - CONTINUOUS - NIGHT 1
The Police Officer finishes patting Slim down.
Have you been drinking tonight? No.
SLIM Slim laughs.
Why is that funny?
Cause I don't drink.
Do me a favor, pop the trunk. Why?
SLIM
Because I asked you to.
Slim hesitates.
Ain't nothing in there but some shoe boxes.
Let's see 'em. Okay.
SLIM Queen HONKS the horn in an effort to get the Police Officer's attention.
Can I help you?
Do you have a warrant?
Ma'am, he's already agreed to the search. So I don't need one.
Slim pops the trunk.
bunch of sneakers. Consumerism is his only vice. Slim feels vindicated and violated at the same time.
SLIM
Can we go now?
The Officer's cheeks turn bright red.
What's in the boxes?
Shoes. SLIM
I'm gonna check just to be sure. Fine.
SLIM
Can you please hurry up?

And just like that a SWITCH GOES OFF in the Police Officer's head. He feels disrespected. His uniform isn't as intimidating as he thought it was. His super powers are imaginary. So he goes from 2018 to 1953 real quick. He PULLS OUT HIS GUN and AIMS IT AT SLIM.
Get on the ground and put your hands behind your back, now!
Are you serious?
Get on the ground!

Slim does as he's told. The Officer keeps the gun pointed at Slim's head while looking at Slim's car to make sure Queen stays inside. But after a beat she GETS OUT OF THE CAR to see what the fuck is going on.
Why is he under arrest?
The Officer turns around and AIMS HIS GUN AT HER. With one hand. The other hand stays planted on Slim's shoulder.
Ma'am if you don't get back in the car I'm gonna have to arrest you too.
I'm happy to get back in the car, but would you mind telling me why you're arresting him?
Get back in the vehicle now!
I'm reaching for my cell phone -POLICE OFFICER Keep your hands where I can see
I have the right to record this arrest.

Queen REACHES in her pocket. Officer Reed AIMS HIS GUN AT HER and FIRES OFF TWO SHOTS. BOOM! BOOM! The first shot misses her, but the SECOND SHOT GRAZES HER RIGHT THIGH. She collapses to the ground. And hits her head on the pavement. Slim SCREAMS for her. He tries to run toward her, but Officer Reed grabs him by the shoulders and slams him on the ground. Officer Reed and Slim roll around on the ground for a few seconds. Until Officer Reed finally pins Slim to the ground and points the gun at his face.
Don't move!

Officer Reed sees Queen trying to get up. He points his gun at her again - while the Officer's attention is diverted Slim KNOCKS the firearm out of his hand. When the gun goes flying - Officer Reed reaches for it, but Slim gets to it first. When Slim turns around he sees Officer Reed LUNGING toward him. Slim FIRES the gun! BOOM. HE SHOOTS OFFICER REED IN THE NECK. Killing him instantly. Slim drops the gun and stops breathing. Queen muffles her own scream. Officer Reed's lifeless body lies on the pavement. A pool of blood quickly forms around him. Queen applies pressure to her fresh wound and winces in pain. Slim gets up and runs to her side. He sees the blood running down her leg.
SLIM He tries to help her up, but she doesn't want his assistance. I got it.
She slowly stands up. Gritting her teeth the entire time. Independence hurts. Queen looks at the dead Police Officer.
Are you okay? Physically yes. Emotionally no. Yeah.

SLIM Slim puts Queen's arm around his neck. He knows she's struggling to stand on one good leg. She kneels down and grabs the gun. Then she gently shoves it in his pants. It's their gun now. Queen turns to face Officer Reed's squad car. The headlights are still on. Giving the small CAMERA on the DASHBOARD a perfect view of her face. Let's go.
QUEEN
Slim looks at her for a long beat. SLIM We can't just leave him here.
Yes, we can. Now let's go.

Slim walks Queen back to the car. The engine is still purring. He helps her into the passenger seat. Slim walks around to the other side and gets in the driver's seat. Still in shock. As they drive off into the darkness all we hear is the static filled radio coming from the squad car.
Officer Reed? Officer Reed, do you need back up? Officer Reed, do you copy?
As we hear sirens far off in the distance, we -CUT TO:
INT. CAR - MOMENTS LATER - NIGHT 1

Slim drives faster than his car is used to going. No music is playing. They're no longer wrapping up a date. Now they're fugitives, on the run. And one of them is wounded. Slim has no idea where they're going. Neither does Queen. He's just driving. Aimlessly. Slim grabs his phone.
I gotta call my dad.
Queen snatches his phone and tosses it out the window. She chucks hers as well.
What the fuck are you doing?
We can't call anybody. It'll make them accomplices.
You could've just said that shit. Now we ain't got no phones.
A phone ain't nothing but a tracking device anyway.
Slim is shell shocked as Queen tries to take deep breaths. SLIM
It's okay it only grazed me. The severity of what they did starts to sink in.
Fuck! Why didn't you just stay in the car?
So this is my fault?
You should've just let me handle it.
If I didn't get out the car you'd be dead right now.
Slim looks at her wound. There's a big gash.
We gotta get you to a hospital.
No! We can't. Just keep driving. Here --
Slim takes off his jacket while driving.
Use my jacket as a bandage.
She balls it up and presses it against her leg. It hurts like
QUEEN
I have a small bottle of bourbon
under the seat. She looks at him with judgmental eyes.
I thought you didn't drink.
I don't. My cousin stole that from a hotel and left it in my car.
She grabs it and pours some on her open wound. Queen grits her teeth and presses against it to stop the bleeding. Slim's mind starts to race. He can't sit still.
What if he was wearing a body cam? Or had a camera in his car. This shit bout to be all over the fucking news.
That's why we have to keep going.
We can't run forever.
I know that. But right now we have to run until we come up with a better plan.
Well, start throwing out some
Since when am I in charge of coming up with the ideas?
Since you made the decision to run.
We made that decision together.
I don't remember that.
We ain't turning ourselves in, I know that much.
If people watch the tape they'll see what happened.
Yeah - they'll see you shooting a police officer, you idiot! SLIM Man, fuck this!
EXT. STREET - NIGHT 1
Slim pulls the car over. He gets out and just starts walking.
What the fuck are you doing? Are you crazy?
Queen limps out and hurries after him. Slim walks aimlessly up the road.
Where are you going?
I'mma find somebody with a phone so I can call my family.
If you do that they'll know where we are. Great.
SLIM
What if they kill us?
That stops Slim in his tracks. He turns to look at her.
Don't say that.
There's no guarantee they won't. Then.
You're a black man that killed a cop and then you took his gun. SLIM We took it for protection. Exactly.
I'm not a criminal.
You are now.
Slim stares at her.
I just want to go home. I wanna see my family.
Slim's eyes start to water. Queen grabs his hand.
If you turn yourself in - you'll never see them again.
And just like that. Something in Slim shifts. He feels powerless and small.
All we can do is go forward. There's nothing back there for us. Please. Let's just keep going.
What about your family? You don't care about leaving them behind?
No. I don't.
We can't just keep driving. We need to figure out what the fuck we gon do.
Alright, let's go to my uncle's house. We can stay there for a few days. Then we can figure out our next move.
Where does your Uncle live?
New Orleans.
That's hours from here.
Then we should get going.
They slowly walk back to the car. Hand in hand.
INT. SLIM'S CAR - MOMENTS LATER
Slim blasts GOSPEL music. Queen stares out the window. Pissed.
Can you please turn that shit down?
It's the only thing that calms me.
Well I can't hear myself think.
I don't care.
She turns it down.
Don't touch my shit. Pull over. Why?
SLIM
So I can get out.
You ain't going nowhere. We in this together now.
All she can do is fold her arms and roll her eyes - because she knows he's right.
INT. SLIM'S CAR - HOURS LATER - NIGHT 1
Slim and Queen's hearts are still racing. Then the car starts to slow down.
Why you slowing down? SLIM I'm outta gas.
Then the car comes to a complete stop. They can no longer ride on fumes.
Why didn't you say something?
What you mean why ain't I say something? I had other shit on my mind.
They sit there quietly for a beat. Then they see a flash of light. Headlights to be exact.
Okay, we gotta flag this car down.
I hope they black.
(to herself) That's not always a good thing.
They both step out of the car. Nervous as hell.
EXT. STREET - CONTINUOUS - NIGHT 1
As the car gets closer and closer, Queen realizes QUEEN Oh fuck. The driver's wearing a cowboy hat.
SLIM Slim crosses himself. Hoping for the best. The DRIVER is Mexican. His smile is friendly and his demeanor is warm.
Y'all having car trouble?
Uh, yeah, we ran out of gas.
The Driver sees Queen's leg is covered in blood.
Ma'am, are you okay?
Yeah, I accidentally cut myself.
He looks at Slim and wonders if he's stumbled upon a domestic dispute.
Why don't you guys hop in my truck? I've got a first aid kit in my backseat. And there's a gas station up the road. I'll give you a ride.
We don't mind walking.
Come on. I won't bite.
INT. DRIVER'S TRUCK - CONTINUOUS - NIGHT 1
They get in the truck. Slim immediately spots a SHERIFF'S BADGE sitting on the dash. While Queen climbs in the backseat.
What? What?
SLIM
SLIM
Something wrong?
Uh no. I just didn't know you were a sheriff.
Queen looks up. Freaked out.
Oh yeah, I'm off duty today.
He pulls off.
INT. SHERIFF'S TRUCK - MOMENTS LATER

Queen tries to take deep breaths while she bandages herself up. The bleeding has finally stopped. She uses a lot of gauze and tape because she doesn't know when she'll be able to rebandage it. It looks a mess - cause she obviously doesn't know what she's doing. Slim sits up front, sweating bullets.
You know what. We can walk the rest
of the way.
Nah, I'll let this be my good deed for the day. Uh okay.
SLIM
Y'all okay? You seem jumpy.
No, we're fine. We just made a wrong turn somewhere and got lost. SLIM Where are we?
You're in Kentucky, my friend, but don't worry - we'll get you some gas and you'll be on your way. (then) How's your leg?
Much better. Thank you.
EXT. GAS STATION - MOMENTS LATER - NIGHT 1
The Sheriff looks at them for a beat. They stare back, confused.
Gas costs money.
Oh shit, I left all my stuff in the car. Me too.
I can pay you back when we get to the car.
The Sheriff takes pity on them and walks into the gas station.
INT. GAS STATION - COUNTER - CONTINUOUS - NIGHT 1
The Sheriff grabs a red jug for the gas to go in and then he walks up to THE CASHIER.
I need to fill this up.
That'll be ten bucks.
The Sheriff hands him the money. Then he looks out the window and sees Queen and Slim standing by his car. A VOICE from his walkie gives him an update. VOICE A Police Officer was shot and
He stares daggers at Queen and Slim. They can feel his eyes on them.
EXT. GAS STATION - SAME TIME - NIGHT 1
Queen has her hand on the passenger door. Slim paces back and forth.
Stop pacing.
I can't help it.
You look guilty.
I am guilty.
Have you been charged with something? No.
SLIM
Then you're not guilty. SLIM
Why is he staring at us like that?
I don't know.
INT. GAS STATION - COUNTER - CONTINUOUS - NIGHT 1
The Cashier hands the Sheriff a receipt.
Do you need anything else?
Hold on a second.
He grabs his walkie and talks into it.
What kind of car were they in? VOICE A white Honda.
Any other info?
We're still waiting for the dash cam footage to come in.
EXT. GAS STATION - NIGHT 1
Get in the driver's seat.
Are you crazy?
Just do it. Quick.
Slim hops into the driver's seat. Queen gets in the passenger side. Before Slim can turn the key - BAM! The Sheriff holds the red jug up to the driver side window. Startling them.
Were y'all gon leave without me? And before he can blink - Queen holds the dead police officer's gun to his head.
Get in the car.
The Sheriff does as he's told. Without alarming anyone.
INT. SHERIFF'S TRUCK - MOMENTS LATER
The Sheriff is driving while Queen holds a gun to the back of his head from the backseat. Slim is in the passenger seat wishing he could make himself disappear.

My name is Edgar. I've lived in Kentucky all my life. My wife died three years ago and now I'm raising our son by myself. I'm all he has in the world. Watching him grow up has been the greatest blessing of my life.
Don't worry. I'm not gonna kill you.
After a beat.
Did y'all kill that police officer? SLIM It was self defense.
I'm sure it was. I have some colleagues that have gotten out of line more than once.
EXT. STREET - CONTINUOUS - NIGHT 1

They're back at Slim's car. Queen keeps the gun to his head. The Sheriff keeps his hands where she can see them. Queen looks a the Sheriff's truck and then she looks at Slim's car. Then -QUEEN Pop the trunk. SLIM
Just do it.
Slim does as he's told. Then she looks at The Sheriff.
Get inside, please.
The Sheriff struggles to get inside Slim's trunk. There's a lot of shoeboxes in there. He finally gets inside. Once he's in the trunk Queen leans over him.
I don't know what happened. But whatever it was, I'm sure you had good reason for doing what you did. Please. Let me help you.
Queen just looks at him, with hurt and anger in her eyes and SLAMS the trunk closed.
What'd you do that for? He wanted to help us.
He ain't gon help us.
Slim takes a deep breath trying to calm himself down.
It's their job to make you feel like everything will be okay. But the second you confess you become property of the state. Is that what you want? You wanna be the state's property?
Queen gets in the driver's seat of the pick up truck and leans her head out the window.
You coming?
Slim hops in the truck - and they're off.
INT. TRUCK - MOMENTS LATER - NIGHT 1
Queen is speeding down the highway.
SLIM
I'm just tryna keep us from getting pulled over again.
Queen wants to run away so bad. That's her pattern. But she can't this time. She's trapped.
I'm hungry. Me too.
SLIM He sees a few fast food spots up ahead.
Just stop at McDonalds or something.
That's disgusting.
Now ain't the time to be choosy.
EXT. MCDONALDS DRIVE THRU - FORTY MINUTES LATER - NIGHT 1 21

Queen spots a few BROWN BOYS failing miserably at skateboard tricks in the parking lot. She sits up in her seat and locks eyes with one of them. He has a lot of meat on his bones and a face covered in freckles. We'll call him CHUBBY. She WAVES him over. Slim clocks this and freaks out.
What you doing?
The BOYS don't even try to stop Chubby from walking over. They just keep busting their asses on the pavement.
You wanna get some free food and make a little money?
What's the catch?
Ain't no catch, lil man. Aight.
CHUBBY Queen hands him the last twenty dollar bill from her wallet. SLIM
with sweet and sour sauce.
I'll have a chicken sandwich with no condiments and a small fry. You can keep the change.
Ain't gon be much left after all that.
EXT. TRUCK - MOMENTS LATER - NIGHT 1
They're sitting on the bed of the truck - under the stars. Slim eats fast and loud. Queen eats like a rabbit. She watches him devour his food with a disgusted look on her face.
You make a lot of noise when you eat.
Woman, this could be my last meal would you just let me be?
It's hard for me to eat with you making so much noise.
Slim tries to eat quieter. He fails, but Queen appreciates the gesture.
I've never heard of someone not being able to eat because they're disgusted by the sounds of other
people eating.
I know it's weird, but I have a thing about it.
How do you avoid the sounds of
people eating?
I eat alone most of the time.
That's not surprising.
She cuts her eyes at him. Slim stops eating for a second.
You scared? No.
You're lying.
How do you know?
You answered too fast.
I thought if a person answered too fast it meant they were telling the truth.
My dad always told me if a person answers too quick it means they had the lie ready to go.
Wow. Well that's good to know.
How do you know if your clients are telling the truth? I don't.
Then how can you defend them?
That's my job.
But why do you do it?
I refuse to let certain states swap out a southern tree for an electric chair and call it justice. A beat.
Are you a good lawyer?
I'm an excellent lawyer.
Why do black people always feel the need to be excellent? Why can't we just be ourselves?
We should go.

She hops down and gets in the passenger side. Slim wonders why she was so abrupt just then. Maybe he struck a nerve. He shakes it off and hops in the drivers seat. But before they can pull off he hears -CHUBBY (O.S.)
Chubby plays them a video on his phone. It's the DASH CAM FOOTAGE of Slim shooting Officer Reed in the neck. The video has already been viewed by over a hundred thousand people.
Nah, man. That ain't us.
Slim gets spooked and quickly starts the car. Chubby keeps banging on the window.
Hold on, my dad wanna talk to y'all!
As soon as he pulls forward he feels an IMPACT. He HIT
CHUBBY (CONT'D)
Slim gets out of the car. Queen stays inside. Slim spots a LARGE BLACK MAN laying on the ground. Slim goes to touch him, but he flinches.
I'm so sorry. I didn't see you.
You almost killed my daddy!
I think you hit my bad knee.
Can you move it?
He tries to and then he SCREAMS in pain. Chubby starts crying.
You broke my daddy leg!
Stop crying like a lil bitch. I'll be alright.
Mommy told you to stop calling me a lil bitch.
Nigga, I'll call you whatever the fuck I want.
Queen hops out of the car to move this along.
We have to go now.
Y'all gotta take him to the hospital.
We can't go to a hospital.
So I can get y'all food, but you can't take my daddy to the
hospital?
Large Black Man moans in pain every time he tries to move his leg.
Where's the nearest hospital?
We don't have time for this.
Would you calm down!
Don't talk to me like that.
I'll talk to you however I wanna talk to you.
Ya'll sound like me and my bitch.
Don't call my mama a bitch!
I ain't talking bout yo mama.
Slim tries to help the Large Black Man to his feet.
Come on, we're gonna take you to a hospital.
Slim, Queen, and Chubby all grab a limb and help him into the back of the pick up truck.
INT. TRUCK - CONTINUOUS - NIGHT 1
Slim drives carefully as to not irritate the Large Black Man's injury. Chubby holds his dad's hand the whole way. LARGE BLACK MAN I support what y'all are doing.
What do you mean? Killing these crooked ass cops. We need to take all these muthafuckas out.
That's not what we're doing.
I saw the video, okay. If a cop shot at my bitch, I'd kill his ass too.
Queen and Slim look at each other. Their hearts are racing. LARGE BLACK MAN (CONT'D)
Shut the fuck up, man!
Are y'all the new Black Panthers!? Is that what this is? No!
This is revenge. For what?
SLIM LARGE BLACK MAN
QUEEN

Queen curls up in a ball and closes her eyes. Slim stares off into the night. While he looks up at the stars, he hears -QUEEN I can't sleep.
You want me to turn on the radio?
No. I want you to tell me a story.
He turns to look at her. Confused and annoyed.
What kind of story?
Any story. I don't care.
He fidgets with his hands as he begins to speak.

When I was little I asked my mom where babies come from. She said I was too young to know. So I went asked my dad. He said babies are the product of two people that needed something from each other. I didn't know what that meant. So I said -- needed what? He said maybe they needed to feel seen. Maybe they needed to feel loved. Maybe they needed to feel human.
And then what?
Then I asked my grandmother and she said -- babies come from God. They're His way of making sure no one ever really dies. I remember her saying "through our children we are reborn."
Queen smiles.
Then I went to my older brother and asked him and he said, nobody knows. You're just born. That's it. Now leave me alone. Slim chuckles at the memory.
I wasn't satisfied with that answer either, but I'd run out of family members to ask - so he got the last word.
So whose right?
Tears well up in Slim's eyes. He wonders if he'll ever see them again.
None of 'em. Babies come from fucking. Plain and simple.

They both chuckle. Then Queen's eyelids become heavy. She eventually closes her eyes and falls asleep. Slim stares at her. Grinning. Watching her sleep has quickly become one of his favorite pastimes. After a moment he finds it hard to keep his eyes open as well. Soon - they're both fast asleep. In a station wagon. In the middle of a field. Somewhere in Florida.
INT. STATION WAGON - DAY 7 (DAWN)

Slim is in a deep sleep. Mouth open. Head tilted. Then we hear - CLICK CLACK. Slim JOLTS awake. There's a RIFLE aimed at Slim's face. Once his brain computes what's happening he scoots over into the drivers seat - this causes Queen to wake up too.
What's going on?

She looks at the man holding the rifle. He's a BLACK MAN with a scruffy BEARD and inquisitive eyes. He's wearing an oversized white t-shirt and his teeth are covered in 14 karat GOLD grillz. Queen and Slim aren't sure if they should feel anxious or at ease.
Why y'all acting all scared and shit?
Cause you pointing a gun at my face?
He joyfully swings it over his shoulder.
Oh I'm just fucking wit you, playa.
He opens the door on the driver's side. Slim gets out, cautiously.
You know the Shepherds? Hell yeah.
They were supposed to call you.
Y'all the ones that killed the cop, right? Slim doesn't want to confess to this.
I got you. Follow me.
He walks pretty fast. Queen and Slim trail behind him. Then -SLIM How do you know the Shepherds?
From way back. How long?
SLIM
Man, I don't know - why you giving me the third degree. I'm just tryna help you.
Queen and Slim try to keep up with him.
They said you had a plane.
They lied about that.
Slim stops his tracks. Queen and the Thin Black Guy stop too.
If you don't have a plane we not going with you.
Look man, I ain't got that kind of money. But I got a friend that do. (MORE)
He a baller. He can get whatever you want. When we get to my trailer Imma call him.
Why can't you call him right now?
Cause I ain't got my phone on me.
Queen looks skeptical.
Look, y'all can either roll with me or stand here like some dummies and wait for the police to come get you.
INT. TRAILER - MOMENTS LATER - DAY 7
Queen and Slim sit in the Black Man's tiny trailer. He's smoking a blunt while he wraps up his call.
Aight - he gon have the plane ready to go first thing tomorrow morning.
We can't wait until tomorrow. We gotta go now.
Damn, y'all some bossy muthafuckas.
Call him back.
Slim grabs the cordless phone and hands it to him.
(on the phone) Sorry bro, they trippin. They said they gotta go today, nigga. That's what I told 'em. Aight. Bet.
He looks at Queen and Slim and shrugs his shoulders. Then he hangs up.
He said meet him in a hour.
Slim and Queen settle in.
What the fuck is y'all doing? It's a hour away - we gotta go.
INT. TRUCK - MOMENTS LATER - DAY 7
Queen and Slim sit in the backseat holding hands while the bearded Black Man drives. He rolls another blunt while he drives. He's great at multitasking.
I don't smoke. Word?
What about you, sis? I'm good.
Shit, if I was y'all I'd be smoking like a muthafucka.
He lights the blunt and brings it to his lips and blows out a large cloud of smoke.
That's why I smoke all the time. Shit, I'm paranoid as fuck. I always think somebody tryna kill me. Who?
Police. Niggas. Everybody.
That's no way to live.
What you gon do? That sticks to Slim's ribs. Yeah.
SLIM
I'm glad I met y'all, though.
He reaches out his hand for Slim to grab. They quickly dap each other up.
So are we, man.
Y'all really gave niggas something to believe in.
This brings a proud grin to Slim's face.
We needed that. For real.
Queen reaches forward and grabs the blunt out of the Bearded Black Man's hand. She takes a huge hit. As she blows it out -BLACK MAN (CONT'D)
They all laugh.
Live yo life, sis!
Then she passes it to Slim. Nudging him to do it. He waves her away at first.
Come on. For me.
That does it. Slim takes the blunt and hits it a little too hard. They both laugh at him. Then the Bearded Black Man looks out the window and sees a small PLANE up ahead.
There that nigga go.
EXT. PLANE - DAY 7
It's the kind of plane famous people get on to die. He pulls up along side it. Queen and Slim hop out. They both shake his hand with gratitude. BLACK MAN

Queen grabs Slim's hand and they both start walking toward the plane. It's been a long road to this point. They're ready to leave it all behind. Then -They can hear HELICOPTERS floating above them. They both look up. Fuck! They turn around to find FIVE SQUAD CARS lined up. TWO COPS step out of each vehicle. GUNS DRAWN. The Bearded Black Man with the kind eyes was a Judas after all. One of the Police Officers gets on the bullhorn.
Get on the ground now! Put your
hands behind your head. Queen and Slim look at each other. Never letting go of each other's hand.
Get on the ground now!
There's no getting out of this, is there?
Slim says "no" with his eyes. Queen looks up at the sky - and somehow manages to muster a smile.
Well. I'm not letting go of your hand.
If I had the chance I would've kissed all your scars.
I know you would have.
Queen looks at him with tears in her eyes.
Can I be your legacy?
You already -BOOM!

A BULLET - traveling faster than the speed of light - pierces through Queen's chest. Causing her body to DROP. And just like that... a complex beauty with beautiful brown skin and a tortured past is gone. Slim kneels down and takes her in his arms. He stands up holding her lifeless body. With tears streaming down his face he walks toward the police slowly and deliberately. The police are stunned and confused. What the fuck is he doing? It doesn't matter. All we know, is his death is imminent.
Stop walking!
He doesn't.
Put your hands up, now!
He doesn't.
Stop or we'll shoot!

He keeps walking. Defiantly. Then -- BOOM! BOOM! Two bullets BLOW through his chest. He plummets to the ground. Never letting go of her. He died just as he had lived - with pride and a quiet dignity. They both lay there on the pavement. In a pool of their own blood. Their bodies making an unintentional cross. The COPS put their guns down. Feeling powerful and guilty at the same time. It's an adrenaline rush they can't describe. The Police Officers look at Queen and Slim's dead bodies. Observing them. Not unlike a hunter observes his prey.
INT. VARIOUS LOCATIONS - DAY 7

We see thousands of people glued to their TV and iPhone screens as they watch what just took place. This is public theater. It's like the OJ Simpson verdict all over again. Only this time black people are on the losing end.
NEWS ANCHOR (V.O.)
Angela Johnson and Ernest Hinds

We'll see THE SHEPHERDS watching from home. Mrs. Shepherd watches with conflicted eyes. Mr. Shepherd has to look away. We'll see GODDESS and NAOMI watching on their phones in Earl's overcrowded house. Earl smokes a cigarette and pretends to be unfazed. But he is. He's never felt more alone in the world than he does right now. We'll see Junior's father watching from his shop. He doesn't have much space to mourn other people's children when he's still mourning his own. Finally - we'll see the Bearded Black Man watching. He sits in his trailer with a blunt dangling from his lips while counting his reward money. We'll search for remorse behind his eyes, but we won't find it.
INT. MORGUE - LATER THAT DAY - DAY 7
The floor is covered in blood and water. The CORONER is a dignified black man with salt and pepper
He washes Queen and Slim's bodies as if they were his favorite cousins. He prays over them. He does it in a voice low enough so he won't get in trouble, but loud enough so God will hear him.
EXT. OHIO STREET - THREE DAYS LATER - DAY 9

HUNDREDS OF PEOPLE, mostly Black and Latino, stand in the streets holding flowers with sad eyes. They pour into the streets to pay their respects to two ordinary people that became sacrificial lambs. It's reminiscent of BIGGIE'S funeral. Only this time there's no music being played from boom boxes. There's no animation or joy to be found. We see Slim's beloved father in the crowd. He bows his head and takes off his hat as his son's casket rolls by. Slim's family consists of his OLDER BROTHER, his ELEGANT MOTHER and HIS BROTHER'S CHILDREN - two nephews and one niece. The only person there to grieve QUEEN is Earl. He cries uncontrollably as he reaches out to touch her casket. It's covered in white hydrangeas. Her favorite. YOUNG KIDS hold up the PHOTO Junior took of Queen & Slim in the car. As we linger on the photo and their innocent faces, we -CUT TO:
EXT. STREET - DAY 9

FIVE LITTLE BLACK BOYS, run through an apartment complex. They're making their way to a basketball court. As they play - we notice one of the boys is wearing a t-shirt with Queen and Slim's faces on it. And just like that they've gone from skin and bones to mythical beings that everyone wishes they knew.

Heroes that everyone admires and young people will emulate for generations to come. Whether you call them sacrificial lambs, revolutionaries or just two innocent people that defended themselves -- their names will live on forever. But for the purposes of this story -- they will always be known as Queen and Slim.
